quote: “Leave your rank at the door, be prepared to start at the bottom. Your experience in the Military is invaluable, but with a new profession you are going to have learn new things, and there is no better place to do that in the trenches. Right now, that’s what I’m doing in the classroom. I’m using my vast leadership experience to help make me successful yet learning everyday about a new profession. O2O helped me get in the right mindset do that.” Military Installation: Fort Carson CISSP Graduate Corpus Christi Catholic School Spanish Teacher, Since August 5, 2018
citation: Dan Walker Jr. Major, US Army

quote: “Perhaps not a quote, but a general philosophy: for each military assignment you had to walk into a new role, new environment, take on new challenges and reach new summits. Transitioning to the civilian world is no different. Use your hard-earned skills of research, reconnaissance and observation and be willing to learn from those around you and change your approaches to getting what you want and need for you and your family post-military. Colleagues that were inflexible and unwilling to understand and adapt to the civilian world (which is a lot bigger than the military microcosm) were not as successful. The longer you’re not successful, the harder it becomes…sometimes ending up in a vicious circle.” Military Installation: Fort Carson Sec+ Graduate Defense Senior Project Manager, GPS Source, Since September 2018
citation: Bryan Trinkle Lt. Col., US Air Force

quote: “The EBV-A and EDGE programs built upon my previous skills and experiences in civil engineering, software development, and digital marketing. Upon completing the programs, the IVMF continued to support me through their Post Program Support resources. I took advantage of their alumni network, ongoing mentorship opportunities, and access to additional training sessions. These services eased my transition and significantly benefited my business by providing continuous learning and networking opportunities. My experience with the IVMF has been incredibly positive. The organization’s dedication to empowering veteran entrepreneurs is evident in the quality of their programs and the ongoing support they provide. I am grateful for the knowledge, skills, and connections I gained through the EBV-A and EDGE programs, which have been pivotal in the growth and success of Patriot Conceptions.” IVMF Program Alumni: EDGE ’23, ’24 | EBV-A ’23 | VPPCE ’23 | CEOCircle ’24

quote: “I got laid off from my former work this February. I wanted to change my career path but didn’t know where to start. My husband, who is a veteran, recommended this program which he found on Facebook. Luckily, I was able to be part of Onward to Opportunity that started in March. I was aiming for CompTIA A+ certification since I had one experience in that field as an assistant. However, that was years ago and while I was working as an administrative assistant and a manager, so many things have changed. I thought I was able to pass two tests much faster than the deadline but there were so many new things that I didn’t know about, it took me 6 months. During that time, the program manager, advisor, and coordinator were all there to answer my questions. I really appreciate it. Moreover, this program will pay for your exam! I didn’t want to waste their vouchers so I studied really hard. I’m so glad that I got in this program. Thank you so much!” Joint Base Lewis-McChord CompTIA A+ (2021 Graduate)
html:
